turns.user_message: I used to breeze through unexpected changes without a second thought, but lately even the smallest shift in my routine leaves me completely rattled. I catch myself holding my breath over things that never used to bother me at all. My chest gets tight and my thoughts start spinning out of control before I can even catch myself. It is just a weird feeling to watch my own reactions spiral over absolutely nothing. I keep waiting for it to pass, but the tension just lingers in the background.

What really gets to me is how this reaction has become my new normal instead of just a passing phase. I used to just shake off that nervous energy, but now it builds up until I feel completely overwhelmed by my own heartbeat. Every little surprise feels like a warning sign that something is about to go wrong. I try to ground myself by taking deep breaths, but the panic just keeps creeping back in anyway. It is exhausting to constantly monitor my own body for signs of trouble.

I honestly do not recognize myself anymore because this constant state of alert has completely taken over my everyday life. I used to laugh at people who got worked up over minor details, but now I am the one trapped in that exact cycle. My mind races so fast that I feel like I am about to jump out of my own skin. Every single moment feels heavy and urgent, leaving me completely drained before the day even really starts. I just want to go back to feeling steady and calm without having to fight for it.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens by contrasting with how things used to be. This used to not bother the speaker.
